Pricing

How much does a website or web app cost?

Ballpark prices are public — you get an exact, fixed quote within 48 hours after a short call. No hidden costs: what we agree is what you pay.

Prices are indicative and depend on project scope. I work directly, without agency overhead — the same work costs less, and you talk to the person who actually writes the code.

Business website

WordPress or a modern static site for companies that need a serious online presence.

from €450

1–2 weeks

  • Up to 7 pages, design matched to your brand
  • Responsive — mobile, tablet, desktop
  • Base SEO setup and Google indexing
  • Contact form, map, social links
  • CMS — edit texts and images yourself
  • Handover training (1h call)
Request a quote →

Booking website

A booking system for apartments, tours, transport or services — guests book online, you manage everything from a panel.

from €900

2–4 weeks

  • Online reservations with availability calendar
  • Admin panel for managing bookings
  • Email confirmations and notifications
  • Multilingual (e.g. English + German + Serbian)
  • Online card payments (optional)
  • Seasonal pricing and discounts
Request a quote →

Custom web application

A Laravel + React system built around your business — from ticket sales to internal tools and CRMs.

from €2,500

4–10 weeks

  • Laravel + MySQL backend, React frontend
  • Admin panel with roles and permissions
  • Integrations: payments, fiscal receipts, email/SMS
  • API ready for a mobile app
  • Reports and exports (CSV/Excel)
  • Server setup + monitoring
Request a quote →

Online store

WooCommerce or custom e-commerce — products, cart, card payments and shipping.

from €800

2–4 weeks

  • Product catalog with variations
  • Card payments and cash on delivery
  • Stock and order management
  • Coupons, discounts and newsletter
  • Courier service integrations
  • Base product SEO
Request a quote →

Maintenance & support

from €50 / per month

After launch you're not on your own. A monthly plan covers updates, backups, monitoring and small changes — the site stays fast, secure and up to date.

  • Regular updates and security patches
  • Daily backups and uptime monitoring
  • Small content changes (texts, images, prices)
  • Priority support — same-day response

What drives the price?

So you know upfront where the budget goes — no surprises in the quote.

Scope of functionality

The number of pages and features is the biggest factor — bookings, payments and admin panels take more work than a brochure site.

Custom design vs. theme

A theme adapted to your brand is faster and cheaper; design from scratch gives a unique look but costs more.

Integrations

Card payments, fiscal receipts, couriers, booking channels (Booking.com/Airbnb) — each integration adds scope.

Multiple languages

A site in two or three languages means more content and per-language SEO setup (hreflang, translations, localization).

Content readiness

If your texts and photos are ready, you save time and money. I can help with structure and copywriting.

Deadlines

Standard timelines are included in the price. Rush delivery is possible for a surcharge when the schedule allows.

How working together looks

  1. 1

    Free call

    30 minutes — we go through what you need, budget and timeline. No obligations.

  2. 2

    Fixed quote in 48h

    You get an exact price, scope and a phased delivery plan. The price does not change afterwards.

  3. 3

    Development in phases

    I work in weekly phases with reviews — you see progress and give feedback along the way.

  4. 4

    Launch + 30 days of support

    Server setup, training and a month of free fixes after launch.

Pricing FAQ

A business website is typically €450–900, a booking website €900–2,500, and a custom web application starts around €2,500. The exact price depends on features, integrations and content — after a short call I send a fixed quote, so you know the exact amount before we start.

Fixed. After the call you get a quote with an exact amount and scope — that is what you pay. If you want extra features beyond the agreed scope, we price them separately and upfront.

A business website takes 1–2 weeks, a booking website 2–4 weeks, and a custom web application 4–10 weeks. Timelines also depend on how quickly texts, photos and feedback arrive.

Yes — payment is split by phases: a deposit to start (usually 30–40%), a part at review and the rest at delivery. For bigger projects we agree a schedule that fits your cash flow.

A logo (if you have one), texts and photos, and a clear picture of what the site should do. If something is missing, I help with structure, copywriting and photo selection.

You work directly with the person writing the code — no middlemen, account managers or agency margins. Same quality (5+ years of production systems, 150,000+ tickets processed), faster communication and a noticeably lower price.

Yes. Monthly maintenance from €50 covers updates, backups, monitoring and small changes. The first 30 days after launch, fixes are free with or without a maintenance plan.

Ready for an exact quote?

Tell me what you need — within 48 hours you get a fixed quote and a phased delivery plan.
